mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows, providing a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers. It is ideal for developers looking to create AI applications quickly and efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential complexity in deployment.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Langflow CLI tool to manage your Langflow applications.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Ensure you have Python 3.10-3.13 installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command sets up your local kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
Deploy the Langflow application using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Install Langflow in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Langflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 --namespace langflow

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Ensure you have a monitoring configuration file ready.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware of potential costs associated with running your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud compute instances list

```

> Check the running instances and their costs.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Langflow deployment and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the Langflow deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace langflow > backup-langflow.txt

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl get pods --namespace langflow

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 0.0.0.0:443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure the cluster is running with 'gcloud container clusters list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: chart requires Kubernetes version 1.26.0 - 1.30.0'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes version is incompatible with the Langflow chart.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster is running a supported version (1.26 - 1.30).

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may not be starting correctly due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace langflow' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved visual builder interface.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.7.0, ensure to update your API calls to the new endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
